food_subgroup: Hot tea beverages — sweetened black tea

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general black tea beverage class (FOODON_03301103). No specific entry exists for Teh O Gao (concentrated sweetened black tea). Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJX covers black tea infusion generically. No specific code exists for the Singaporean Teh O Gao preparation with added sugar. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34728003 refers to tea as a substance. No specific concept for Teh O Gao exists. Closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cup of Teh O Gao served at a Singapore kopi tiam is approximately 200 ml, containing concentrated black tea brewed at double strength and sweetened with sugar, with no milk added.

nutrient_source.title: Sweetened black tea (Teh O) — HPB Singapore Foodo / USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: Tea, black, brewed, prepared with tap water (NDB 14355) adapted for sugar addition
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Plain brewed black tea per HPB Foodo and USDA SR Legacy (NDB 14355) provides the base. Sugar addition is estimated at approximately 8 g per 100 ml of beverage (typical Singapore kopi tiam preparation for 'Gao' / concentrated style), yielding approximately 32 kcal per 100 g, 8 g carbohydrate, and 8 g sugar per 100 g. Protein and fat remain effectively zero. Sodium is trace from tea. Values are consistent with HPB Singapore nutrient data for sweetened teh o.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; sweetened tea / sugar-sweetened beverages analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for Teh O Gao specifically. GI is estimated at 45 based on the GI of sucrose (GI ~65) diluted in a predominantly water-based beverage with low carbohydrate density (8 g/100 ml). Sweetened tea beverages in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 tables show GI values in the range 40–55 depending on sugar concentration. A GI of 45 is used as a conservative mid-range estimate for a moderately sweetened black tea. GL per serving = (45 × 16 g carbohydrate per 200 ml serving) / 100 = 7.2.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (3 mg per 100 g); no added salt
hypertension.guidance: Teh O Gao is very low in sodium (approximately 6 mg per 200 ml serving) and poses minimal direct risk for hypertension; however, individuals should be mindful of total daily sugar intake from sweetened beverages as part of an overall dietary pattern.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar contributing approximately 16 g of free sugars per 200 ml serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit or avoid Teh O Gao due to its free sugar content (approximately 16 g per serving), which can cause postprandial blood glucose elevation; requesting less sugar ('siu dai') or switching to unsweetened Teh O is strongly recommended.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible fat and saturated fat; moderate free sugar intake
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Teh O Gao contains negligible fat and saturated fat, posing low direct cardiovascular risk from lipid intake; however, regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with increased cardiovascular risk, and reducing sugar content is advisable for cardiovascular health.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Teh O Gao (also written Teh O Kaw or Tea Gao) is a popular Singaporean kopi tiam beverage consisting of strong, concentrated black tea brewed at double or triple the normal strength, sweetened with sugar, and served without milk. The name derives from Hokkien: 'Teh' means tea, 'O' means black (no milk), and 'Gao' (厚) means thick or concentrated. It is a staple of Singapore's traditional coffee shop (kopi tiam) culture and is commonly ordered alongside kaya toast or soft-boiled eggs. The beverage has a robust, slightly astringent flavour with a pronounced tannin character balanced by sweetness from added sugar. It is typically served hot in a ceramic cup or glass. Nutritionally, Teh O Gao provides approximately 32 kcal per 100 g, with carbohydrate and sugar as the primary macronutrients from added sugar; protein and fat are negligible. The beverage contains naturally occurring tea polyphenols and antioxidants from black tea. For individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, the free sugar content (approximately 16 g per 200 ml serving) warrants caution and sugar reduction is recommended. Sodium content is very low, making it suitable for hypertension management when sugar is controlled. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 45 (low GI) due to the dilution effect of the water-based beverage, though the glycemic load per serving is moderate at approximately 7.2.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Teh O Gao?
Is Teh O Gao suitable for diabetics?

Can I drink Teh O Gao if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: No exact HPB Foodo entry for Teh O Gao as a distinct product was available at time of processing. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy plain brewed black tea (NDB 14355) with sugar addition estimated at 8 g per 100 ml based on standard Singapore kopi tiam preparation conventions. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. Confidence is medium rather than high due to the adapted rather than exact source match.
notes: 1. Cuisine corrected from 'Italian' (input error) to 'Singaporean' — Teh O Gao is a quintessential Singapore kopi tiam beverage with Hokkien-origin naming. 2. fat_g is 0.0: naturally zero, as black tea brewed without milk contains no fat by nature. 3. saturated_fat_g is 0.0: naturally zero for the same reason. 4. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: naturally zero as this is a liquid beverage with no fibre-containing solids. 5. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: naturally zero as this is a plant-based beverage with no animal products. 6. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for brewed black tea. 7. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g are 0.0: naturally zero as no fat is present. 8. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for plain brewed black tea. 9. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 10. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 11. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 12. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 13. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 14. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 15. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 16. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 17. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 18. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 19. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 20. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 21. glycemic_index set to 45 (low GI) as an analogous estimate for a moderately sweetened black tea beverage; gi_category low per GI threshold rules (45 ≤ 55). 22. GL per serving = (45 × 16 g carbohydrate in 200 ml serving) / 100 = 7.2.
